programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.

programmes.show_name: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan presents a variety of short programmes focusing on Japan and Asia.
Chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto, and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey of local cuisine served with a side of seasonal scenery at the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama.
Bangladesh has held its first general election since anti-government protests in which 1,400 protestors were killed. We join young voters who carry on the will of those who lost their lives.

This miniseries presents the masterpieces of Tsutaju, a brilliant ukiyo-e producer of the Edo period - this episode features ukiyo-e artist Sharaku's 'The Actor Ichikawa Ebizo as Takemura Sadanoshin'.

programmes.show_name: The Key to a Kitty's Heart
programmes.show_description: This special programme invites us to learn about wildlife photographer Iwago Mitsuaki's craft and the secret to a kitty's heart - Iwago and the cast discuss the incredible tie between humans and cats.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
A 15-minute spin-off of the 'Somewhere Street' series, this programme explores cities around the world.
Cape Ashizuri is marked by rugged cliffs and peculiar rocks. A strong current off its coast has been the cause of many accidents. Enjoy the scenery at the cape, a place of worship, in time-lapse.
Noren director Nakamura Shin draws on the skills of traditional artisans to produce original pieces. We follow him as he explores the history of noren and strives to share its culture with the world.

The nuns of Otowasan Kannonji Temple show us how to make shiba-zuke pickles - cucumbers, myoga, and a little bit of ume vinegar make the perfect side dish to go with newly-harvested rice.
2025 marks the 110th anniversary of the Nagasaki Electric Tramway, which has long been a vital means of transportation for both residents and tourists - discover the charm of Nagasaki's streetcars.

The delicious bonito Tosa is known for spoils easily, and the flavour varies greatly. Fishmongers at a local port know exactly how to spot the best ones. Watch them in action, in time-lapse.

Participants of the Hyoge Festival clown around in fun and silly costumes - it originated as a sly way of thanking a local hero who built reservoirs but was exiled in the 17th century.
A centuries-old eel catching technique. Bridges designed to be submerged in the river. Locals have found ingenious ways to coexist with the Shimanto. See this mighty river in time-lapse.
Akasaka Yogetsu, a priest with an international following, is known for combining beatboxing with Buddhist mantras. He talks about Zen philosophy and the spirit of 'living in the here and now.'

Urban intrusions by bears have increased significantly worldwide in recent years - what's behind this phenomenon, and how is it related to global warming? We focus our investigation on cases in Japan.
Maxine is a university student who joined a school providing educational support and life advice to international students wanting to enter Japanese high school, and this experience changed her life.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Spear squid is immensely popular in squid-loving Japan - tag along with fishers in Hokkaido and savour some regional cuisine, fresh sushi, and a special take on a beloved Italian-inspired inky dish.
The nuns of Otowasan Kannonji Temple show us how to make sesame-covered taro dumplings - simply mash up boiled taro, add cheese, sprinkle with sesame and deep-fry for some tasty autumn goodness.

GLP-1 receptor agonists are becoming a game changer in the treatment of obesity. However, using them without medical supervision is risky. We report on the potential of GLP-1 treatment.

Gojinjo Taiko is a wild and powerful performance by masked drummers - it originated 400 years ago when villagers disguised themselves as oni (demons) to scare away a warlord's army.

Peaches take the spotlight - from June to October, new varieties appear every two weeks letting people enjoy them for months. Big, sweet and juicy, discover the charm of Japan's finest peaches.

In this episode, we learn about the early spring dates of Risshun and Usui - the nuns show us how to make the seasonal specialities of neko-mochi rice cakes and Girls' Day crackers.

The Supreme Court ruled Japan's defunct Eugenic Protection Law as unconstitutional in July 2024 - we look into why many of the victims who were forcefully sterilised remain silent on their suffering.

A novice filmmaker bet everything on a period drama-and won 7 Japan Academy Awards! 'A Samurai in Time' is an against-all-odds story of passion, grit, and samurai spirit.
A unique time-lapse photography project. Shot by pros and non-pros from around the world, on the transience of nature, celestial phenomena, cityscapes and each single moment of everyday life.

Yoshioka Sarasa revives ancient plant-based colors in Kyoto. As natural dyes grow scarce, she preserves their legacy while creating layered works that reveal new dimensions of nature's hues.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Bear encounters are happening more frequently all over Japan. We'll look at new technologies to reduce the danger they pose, such as GPS tracking and AI predictions.
